signals.reasoning: Existing ETH long remains the cleanest thing to manage on the board, and with veto cleared plus a slightly improved mark, there is even less reason to force an exit. This is still a small, manageable long rather than an urgent cleanup problem.
BTC remains broadly stable after the prior squeeze, but it still does not clearly outrank the existing ETH position for management purposes. Clear board helps, but not enough to justify a switch.
SOL is moving with the same broad tape and is not offering a cleaner management profile than ETH. Hold only.
TST is still a noisy momentum name after its squeeze-style move, which makes it less attractive for disciplined management than the live ETH long. No rotation needed.
STBL can still look mechanically interesting, but it remains unstable enough that it does not outrank the existing ETH exposure. Keep it as watch-only.
PEOPLE still looks more like recovery noise than a clean new trend. It stays in the hold bucket while ETH remains the active managed position.
AZTEC is too fresh and too execution-sensitive to justify rotating away from the current ETH hold. Better watched than traded here.
MAVIA can move sharply, but that volatility makes it a lower-quality management switch from the current ETH position. Hold only.
VVV remains too unstable and reactive to trust as a cleaner alternative. The current ETH hold is simpler and more defensible.
MERL is still in rebound mode without becoming a clearly superior opportunity. It does not justify rotating away from the open ETH position.
LDO still has some constructive rebound structure, but not enough separation to justify leaving the existing ETH hold. Stay with ETH management.
